steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims snowball sampling may be superior to purposive sampling for recruiting participants with the desired characteristics. Supporting evidence must show snowball sampling outperforming purposive sampling at that specific task.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — both methods recruited similar numbers': Equal results show no superiority for either method. ✗
  • Choice B — snowball recruited participants without the desired characteristics': Shows snowball selecting the wrong people, which argues against its superiority. ✗
  • Choice C — snowball yielded significantly more participants with the desired characteristics than purposive did': A direct head-to-head comparison showing snowball recruits more qualifying participants — exactly what 'superior' means here. ✓
  • Choice D — focuses on an irrelevant attribute unrelated to desired characteristics': Does not compare the methods on the relevant outcome. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
